what_it_does: Implements a file-backed registry of channel-attachment binding rows: validates attachment types and channel existence, persists id-keyed rows as frontmatter Markdown files, and exposes attach/detach/get/list operations plus a read-time manifest projection grouped by attachment type.
observations: Binding rows are stored as flat Markdown files under channel-memory/channel_attachments/<id>.md with YAML frontmatter and an empty body.
The manifest is computed on read by grouping rows by attachment_type; it is never persisted.
Attachment types are discovered from runtime/attachment_types via AttachmentTypeRegistry and validated fail-loud.
Channel existence is checked by read-only import of cc_channels._read_channel; this module never writes channel records.
Targets are stored verbatim as opaque refs and are not parsed or resolved here.
Non-multi attachment types are enforced at attach time by scanning existing rows for the channel.
A lazy singleton caches the type registry; reset_registry drops and reloads it.
All storage writes use _atomic_write_fsync and reads use lifters.frontmatter._extract.
The public API supports an optional attachments_dir override for testing or alternate storage roots.
standouts: Manifest-as-projection: the channel attachments view is derived from registry rows rather than stored as a channel field.
File-disjoint design: channel records are read-only imports, preventing desync from direct channel mutation.
Registry-driven type validation with live discovery and non-multi constraints.
Opaque target refs keep the binding layer independent of any address-resolution scheme.
conventions: Mirrors cc_board's id-keyed flat Markdown storage pattern.
Uses fail-loud AttachmentError for all validation and I/O failures.
Employs a lazy singleton global for the attachment type registry.
Frontmatter schema is declared as a tuple of keys and rendered explicitly.
Mixes pathlib.Path with os.path operations for directory and file handling.
Docstrings embed design rationale and cross-references to other files.
concerns: reset_registry immediately re-creates the registry, so it is not a pure cache drop.
_channel_exists swallows all import exceptions and returns False, which can hide misconfiguration.
list_attachments performs a full directory scan and frontmatter parse on every call, with no indexing.
detach uses os.unlink directly without atomicity or durability guarantees.
The global _TYPES cache is not thread-safe and is mutated at module level.
No lifecycle cleanup is defined for bindings when a channel or target is deleted elsewhere.
notes: This file is the binding ledger for channels: every attachment is an independent row, and the manifest is computed on read rather than stored.
questions: What fields does an attachment_type record expose beyond multi, and how is AttachmentTypeRegistry implemented?
Which component resolves opaque target refs like board://<id> or session://<id>?
How are binding rows cleaned up when a channel or target is removed in another module?
What is the planned suite.py lane for promoting attachment_type to a first-class corpus registry kind?
Is there a concurrency model for simultaneous attach/detach operations on the same channel?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — it builds a file-discovered binding registry with a projected manifest and keeps channel-record writes out of scope.
apparent_themes: Separation of binding registry from channel record
intention_signals: It tries to make channel attachments an autonomous, append-only binding layer where the channel record stays immutable and the manifest is always a fresh view of the registry.
novelty: The manifest is a runtime projection of binding rows rather than a persisted field, and the module deliberately never writes the channel record.
contribution: It contributes a durable, type-validated, row-oriented attachment store with non-multi constraints and a computed manifest, letting channels accumulate and shed linked targets without mutating channel records.
summary_for_embedding: A file-backed registry for channel-attachment bindings that stores frontmatter rows, validates types and channels, and computes the channel attachments manifest as a projection.
intention_for_embedding: To keep channel attachments as an independent, evolvable binding layer whose manifest is derived from registry rows rather than embedded in channel records.

symbols.does: Exception class for attachment operation failures.
symbols.description: Subclass of RuntimeError raised when attachment validation or I/O fails; mirrors BoardError and enforces fail-loud behavior.

symbols.does: Lazy singleton accessor for the attachment type registry.
symbols.description: Returns the cached AttachmentTypeRegistry, discovering files under attachment_types/ on first use; reset_registry drops the cache.

symbols.does: Drops the cached type registry and immediately reloads it.
symbols.description: Sets the global _TYPES to None and then calls _types_reg() to re-read the attachment_types directory, making newly added type files live.

symbols.does: Returns sorted list of registered attachment type ids.
symbols.description: Convenience wrapper over _types_reg() that returns the sorted keys of the discovered attachment type registry.

symbols.does: Read-only check whether a channel record exists.
symbols.description: Imports cc_channels._read_channel and returns True only if a record is found; swallows import errors and returns False, ensuring this module never writes channel records.
Produces an ISO 8601 timestamp from datetime.now(timezone.utc) for the binding row's added field.
Returns an 'att-' prefixed identifier using the first eight hex characters of a UUID.
Returns the provided override or the default ATTACHMENTS_DIR under channel-memory/channel_attachments.

symbols.does: Resolves the file path for a binding row.
symbols.description: Joins the attachments directory with '<aid>.md' to produce the Markdown storage path.

symbols.does: Serializes a binding record to frontmatter Markdown.
symbols.description: Dumps the record's FRONTMATTER_KEYS fields as YAML between triple-dash fences, leaving the body empty.

symbols.does: Reads a binding row from its Markdown file.
symbols.description: Parses frontmatter from the given path using lifters.frontmatter._extract and returns the metadata dict, or None if missing.
Ensures the attachments directory exists and writes the rendered record via _atomic_write_fsync to the id-keyed Markdown path.

symbols.does: Creates and persists a channel-to-target binding row.
symbols.description: Validates the attachment type against the registry, optionally validates channel existence, enforces non-multi uniqueness, writes a new row, and returns the record.

symbols.does: Deletes a binding row by id.
symbols.description: Reads the row file, removes it with os.unlink, and returns a summary of the detached binding; fails loudly if absent.

symbols.does: Retrieves a single binding row by id.
symbols.description: Reads the Markdown file for the given attachment id and returns the record, raising AttachmentError if not found.

symbols.does: Lists binding rows with optional filters.
symbols.description: Scans the attachments directory, reads every Markdown file, optionally filters by channel and/or attachment_type, and returns rows sorted by added.

symbols.does: Computes the channel attachments manifest as a projection.
symbols.description: Groups all binding rows for a channel by attachment_type and collects their opaque targets into a view dict with channel and count; the manifest is not stored.
